creator: douglas.j.burn@outlook.com
text: I have an HP Chromebook 11 G5. I did install the stable release of FreeBSD 13.4 in January but haven’t been able to rectify the problem of a slowly-responding built-in keyboard which - on the face of it - seems to be the same problem which was solved earlier in this thread. 13.4 stable has John’s patch built in so I’ve been able to set hw.acpi.isa_irq_override_polarity=1 and =0 however neither seems to make a difference to my built-in keyboard being very slow. The problem is that there’a a lag of up to several seconds between when I press a key and when the character appears on on the screen however an external keyboard does work pefectly when plugged in. I remember that the built-in keyboard didn’t work at all with FreeBSD 15. Does anybody have any ideas about what the problem might be?
